channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Music Industry's Hidden Billions: How Kobalt's Kosign Aims to Unlock Royalties for Emerging Artists
item.description: Kobalt's new platform, Kosign, aims to help emerging artists collect their royalties without committing to a long-term publishing contract. The company hopes to unlock billions of dollars in hidden earnings for up-and-coming songwriters and producers.
item.dc:creator: Julia Selinger, Fast Company

item.title: La contratación de CIO pega el estirón: Cómo conseguir un puesto de alto nivel en tecnología en 2025
item.description: Los primeros resultados de la contratación de líderes de TI en 2025 sugieren un mercado sólido. Para algunas empresas de contratación, el crecimiento del empleo para puestos ejecutivos tecnológicos está en su punto álgido.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google rolls out free version of Gemini Code Assist for individuals
item.description: Google is rolling out a free version of Gemini Code Assist, its tool for code completion, code generation, and code chat, with the aim of helping individual developers improve their productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How VR technology is curing loneliness in seniors
item.description: VR transforms senior care: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says immersive tech helps older adults explore and connect via virtual experiences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple TV+: What's Coming in March 2025 - A Comprehensive Guide
item.description: Find out what Apple TV+ has in store for subscribers this March, including new episodes of popular shows and exciting premieres.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is DALL-E 3: everything you need to know about the AI image generator
item.description: What is DALL-E 3 and how does the AI image generator work? We’ve summarized all the basics in this handy guide, including how to create images from text prompts.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Crazy futuristic city being populated by people willing to leave the real world behind
item.description: Woven City is Toyota's experimental city that is a fully functional environment for people to live, work and play while doing research.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple’s hidden white noise feature may be just the productivity boost you need
item.description: As I write this, the most pleasing sound is washing over me—gentle waves ebbing and flowing onto the shore. Sadly, I’m not actually on some magnificent tropical beach. Instead, the sounds of the sea are being generated by my Mac.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Q&A: East Bay AI safety expert talks about the future of the technology
item.description: Artificial intelligence safety expert and Redwood Research CEO Buck Shlegeris explains the different philosophies guiding A.I. advancement.
item.dc:creator: Chase Hunter, The Mercury News

item.title: Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation
item.description: Leo Garciga, the Army’s CIO, said the Unified Network Optimization initiative is helping the service cut costs and duplicative applications. The post Optimization is a biproduct of Army’s network consolidation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Google’s new AI co-scientist aims to speed up the scientific discovery process
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI co-scientist built on its Gemini 2.0 platform, aiming to accelerate scientific research by generating novel hypotheses and refining experiments. While the system shows promise, questions remain about its performance and broader applicability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Oracle extends support for on-prem Oracle Database 19c
item.description: As the status of an on-premises version of Oracle Database 23ai remains in limbo, the company has tossed a bone to its on-prem Oracle Database 19c customers in the form of an extension of Premier Support to Dec. 31, 2029, and Extended Support to Dec. 31, 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: More used Seagate HDDs disguised as new: Fraudsters make it tough to spot the difference
item.description: The controversy surrounding used Seagate hard drives has significantly widened, with fraudsters now targeting the company’s IronWolf and IronWolf Pro NAS drives alongside the previously affected Exos server-grade HDDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World
